limitations: AI investment intensity: Alliant Energy's AI-attributable spend is not quantified or named as a distinct program; the technology capex table is referenced but dollar amounts for AI/automation are not broken out in the excerpts. Score reflects generic automation language only.

AI narrative concreteness: AI is mentioned primarily in the context of cybersecurity threat vectors (adversarial AI use), not as a deployed internal capability. No named AI initiatives or deployment metrics are present.

AI risk disclosure: Risk-section content is present and discusses AI as a cybersecurity threat vector with named threat actors and attack types, qualifying as named/company-specific risk categories, but no quantification or formal governance body for AI risk is disclosed.
The bulk of the filing's AI-relevant content relates to data center load growth as a customer demand driver, not Alliant Energy's own AI adoption or investment.
